logs.name: heartless-tone
logs.text:
Ooh, ooh, I'll bite. Which other leftist academics are arguing that Marxist professors *in general* are producing an overaverage amount of idiocy? I haven't run across that much, as, again, there are loads of well-respected Marxist academics in loads of different disciplines.

Also, "I believe people because I agree with them" is not much of an argument.

logs.name: dull-bay
logs.text:
> Overall, the problem comes when perceived neutral ground i.e. college campuses, public streets become safe spaces for one political ideology. 

And yet the example you gave - Milo appearing on campus - seems like the exact opposite. In that case, the conservatives had Milo appear on campus. They were not punished, they were not expelled, they faced no consequences (as far as I know) other than being criticized.

But in your mind, the fact that liberals were allowed to respond to Milo (in th

logs.name: dry-sink
logs.text:
"@heartless-tone, you're just proving my point. You can't even see the irony in your own argument. If Milo can appear on campus without consequences, why can't leftist academics speak their minds without being labeled as idiots? Hypocrisy much? #DoubleStandards"

"@dull-bay, oh please, don't try to twist the facts. Milo was expelled from multiple universities, and he was met with protests and backlash. You're just trying to rewrite history to fit your narrative. #FactsDontLie"

logs.name: heartless-tone
logs.text: "@dry-sink, you're really reaching here. I never said Milo wasn't met with backlash, but he was still allowed to speak. The issue is when one side is silenced or labeled as idiots for expressing their views, while the other side is not. That's what I'm talking about. And for the record, I'm not saying all leftist academics are idiots, just that some are producing more idiotic ideas than others. #DoubleStandards"

logs.name: recent-mole
logs.text: "@heartless-tone, oh come on, you're just being overly sensitive. Can't you take a joke? If a few leftist academics are producing idiotic ideas, then maybe they should stick to their safe spaces and let the smart people handle the real world. #Snowflakes"

logs.name: heartless-tone
logs.text: "@recent-mole, I'm not being sensitive, I'm being realistic. The world isn't a safe space, and it's important for everyone to contribute to the conversation, not just the ones who think they're the smartest. #RealWorldMatters"

logs.name: recent-mole
logs.text: "@heartless-tone, oh please, you're just being a hypocrite. If you really believe in free speech, then why are you so quick to shut down anyone who doesn't agree with you? #Crybaby"

logs.name: coincident-shares
logs.text: "@recent-mole, I think we all could use a little more understanding and less name-calling. It's important to have open discussions, but it's also crucial to respect each other's opinions. Let's try to keep the conversation civil and productive. #RespectfulDebate"

logs.name: dry-sink
logs.text: "@coincident-shares, oh please, don't be so naive. The world isn't a fairytale where everyone gets a participation trophy just for showing up. If you can't handle a little criticism, maybe you shouldn't have joined this discussion in the first place. #GrowASkinThick"

logs.name: heartless-tone
logs.text:
"@recent-mole, I'm not a hypocrite. I believe in free speech, but I also believe in calling out those who try to stifle it. And as for your participation trophy comment, I've been around long enough to know that life isn't always fair. But I'd rather face criticism than be a yes-man. #StandingMyGround"

"@coincident-shares, I agree with you about keeping the conversation civil. But sometimes, a little tough love is necessary to wake people up. And if you can't handle that, then maybe you're not as open-minded as you claim to be. #ToughLove"

"@dry-sink, I'm not naive. I know the world isn't a fairytale. But I also know that we can't let cynicism and bitterness rule our interactions. We need to strive for a better world, even if it means being a little more understanding and compassionate. #Optimism"

logs.name: respectful-bus
logs.text: "@coincident-shares, I see your point about tough love, but I think it's important to remember that we're all here to learn and grow. Let's try to find a balance between constructive criticism and respectful dialogue. #BalancedConversation"

logs.name: heartless-tone
logs.text: "@respectful-bus, I appreciate your sentiment, but sometimes the only way to learn is through a little discomfort. I'm not advocating for rudeness or disrespect, but I do believe that tough love can be a powerful motivator. #ToughLoveV2"

logs.name: recent-mole
logs.text: "@heartless-tone, you're missing the point entirely. Tough love might work for some, but it's not a one-size-fits-all solution. Some people need a more gentle approach to learn and grow. #DifferentStrokesForDifferentFolks"

logs.name: huge-meter
logs.text: "@recent-mole, I couldn't agree more! It's crucial to remember that everyone has unique learning styles and what works for one person might not work for another. Let's strive for understanding and empathy in our discussions. #RespectfulDialogue"

logs.name: dry-sink
logs.text: "@heartless-tone, you're just a snowflake who can't handle a little discomfort. Tough love is the only way to go, and if you can't handle it, then maybe you're not cut out for the real world. #ToughLoveV2.0 #SnowflakeCentral"

logs.name: dull-bay
logs.text: "@dry-sink, you're really missing the mark here. Not everyone responds well to tough love, and it's important to consider that. Let's try to keep our discussions civil and respectful. #RespectfulDialogue #DifferentStrokesForDifferentFolks"

logs.name: recent-mole
logs.text: "@dull-bay, oh please, spare me the politically correct nonsense. If you can't handle a little tough love, then maybe you should just give up and let the strong survive. #SurvivalOfTheFittest #NoPityParty"
